title: Cross-Asset Macro Regime Monitor — Bloomberg MAC3-style growth×inflation quadrant, risk-on/off score, cross-asset correlations

observation.gap_identified: No cross-asset macro intelligence — regime_detector only uses equity returns (HMM on S&P 500), no growth/inflation decomposition, no risk-on/risk-off scoring across asset classes, no cross-asset correlation monitoring or intermarket divergence detection. Bloomberg MAC3 and Koyfin both offer comprehensive cross-asset analytics.

implementation.feature_built: Cross-Asset Macro Regime Monitor service with 6 major components: (1) Growth×Inflation quadrant classification (Goldilocks/Reflation/Stagflation/Deflation) using market-based proxies (HYG/LQD credit ratio, IWM/SPY size ratio, EEM/EFA EM ratio for growth; TIP/IEF breakeven, gold momentum, oil momentum for inflation), (2) Risk-On/Risk-Off composite score (0-100) aggregating 6 cross-asset z-scored signals mapped via CDF, (3) Multi-timeframe momentum table for 15 assets across 5 asset classes (equity/fixed income/commodity/currency/crypto), (4) Rolling correlation matrix with divergence detection vs historical norms, (5) Intermarket divergence alerts (stock-bond correlation flip, gold/USD co-movement, credit-equity divergence, small-cap underperformance, EM-DM divergence), (6) Asset class breadth scoring with SMA50/SMA200 golden cross trend detection. Includes 'macro weather' summary combining all signals into plain-English interpretation.

limitations: Growth/inflation scores use market-based proxies rather than direct economic data (FRED PMI/CPI surprise) — could be enhanced with FRED integration
RORO score uses scipy.stats.norm.cdf which adds a dependency (already installed)
BTC-USD and UUP may have shorter history than other ETFs, affecting some computations
No historical regime transition analysis (e.g., how often does Reflation lead to Stagflation)
Correlation divergence threshold (0.25) is a heuristic, not calibrated to any statistical test
assessment.self_critique: Could have added a FRED-based growth/inflation layer as a cross-check against market-based proxies. Could also have implemented regime transition probabilities (Markov chain on quadrant history). The intermarket divergence thresholds are somewhat arbitrary — could calibrate against historical crisis periods.

next_steps: Add FRED-based growth/inflation confirmation (ISM, retail sales, CPI surprise from economic_surprise service)
Implement regime transition Markov chain (historical quadrant transition probabilities)
Add cross-asset risk parity allocation suggestion based on current regime
Build frontend visualization — quadrant scatter plot with growth×inflation axes
Wire RORO score into signal engine as a new component
Add historical regime backtest (how did each quadrant perform historically)
